title: eremophila muelleriana
intro: Eremophila muelleriana, also known as the western emu bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Eremophila muelleriana can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in topical applications for skin conditions.
